title: ajuga grandiflora var. ciliata
intro: Ajuga grandiflora var. ciliata, also known as bugleweed, is a plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to certain regions in Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include acting as an antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory agent to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with bugleweed include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce anxiety and stress, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Bugleweed is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used in herbal medicine to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of insomnia and restlessness.
